complete_workflow_guide.overview: Complete end-to-end workflow for the vuln-remediation-concert mode in bob-shell automated execution. This guide follows the 8-step workflow provided by the GitHub Actions workflow prompt.

workflow_prompt_structure.description: The GitHub Actions workflow provides a structured prompt with 8 steps:
workflow_prompt_structure.prompt_format:
Fix the failing unit tests in PR #{pr_number}.
Follow this workflow: 1. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/{org}/{repo}/pull/{pr_number} 2. Understand what package was upgraded and why tests are failing 3. Run the unit tests locally to see the exact failures 4. Fix the code to work with the upgraded package 5. Verify all tests pass 6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: {branch_name} 7. Push the changes 8. Add a comment to the PR documenting what was fixed

substep.action: Parse PR URL from workflow prompt
Fetch PR details using GitHub MCP

substep.information: - PR title and description - CVE being addressed - Package being upgraded (old → new version) - Changed files - Current test status

step.name: understand_changes
step.title: Step 2: Understand Package Upgrade and Test Failures
step.workflow_instruction: Understand what package was upgraded and why tests are failing

substep.examine: - Dependency file changes (requirements.txt, package.json, etc.) - Old version vs new version - Major/minor/patch version change

substep.sources: - Package changelog - Migration guides - Release notes - API documentation

substep.analyze: - What the DevOps engineer already changed - Code modifications made - Test file updates - Configuration changes

substep.identify: - Number of failing tests - Test failure patterns - Error messages if available

step.name: run_tests
step.title: Step 3: Run Unit Tests Locally
step.workflow_instruction: Run the unit tests locally to see the exact failures

substep.capture: - Total tests run - Number of failures - Specific failing test names - Error messages and stack traces - Deprecation warnings

substep.categorize: - Import errors - API signature changes - Attribute errors - Assertion failures - Behavior changes

step.name: fix_code
step.title: Step 4: Fix Code to Work with Upgraded Package
step.workflow_instruction: Fix the code to work with the upgraded package

substep.priority_order: 1. Import errors (blocking) 2. API signature changes (widespread) 3. Deprecated APIs 4. Behavior changes

substep.workflow: 1. Read the file to be modified 2. Identify exact changes needed 3. Use apply_diff for surgical edits 4. Add explanatory comments 5. Run tests to verify fix 6. Move to next issue

substep.principles: - Only change what's necessary - Preserve existing functionality - Don't compromise security fix - Keep changes focused

substep.command: Run full test suite one final time

substep.check: - Package version still upgraded - No security workarounds added - CVE is fixed

step.name: commit_fixes
step.title: Step 6: Commit Fixes to PR Branch
step.workflow_instruction: Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: {branch_name}

substep.action: Extract branch name from workflow prompt
substep.pattern: Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: (.+)
substep.example: Input: "6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask" Extract: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
substep.critical: This is the ONLY branch name to use. Never create a new branch.

substep.verify: - All changes are intentional - No unrelated modifications - Changes are minimal

substep.check: - Command completed without errors - Commit was pushed to remote

substep.automatic_triggers: - PR is updated with your commit - GitHub triggers CI/CD workflow - Unit tests are re-run - Reviewers are assigned based on test results

step.name: document_fixes
step.title: Step 8: Add Comment to PR
step.workflow_instruction: Add a comment to the PR documenting what was fixed

use_mcp_tool.arguments:
{ "owner": "highorbit25", "repo": "concert-bob-remediate", "pull_number": 38, "event": "COMMENT", "body": "## Unit Test Fixes

I've fixed the failing unit tests caused by the Flask upgrade.

### Changes Made
- Updated import path from `flask.json` to `flask`
- Modified test assertions to use `request.get_json()`

### Root Cause
The upgrade from Flask v2.0.1 to v3.1.0 introduced these breaking changes:
- `flask.json` module was removed
- `request.json` property deprecated

### Testing
- All 3 unit tests now pass ✅
- No regressions introduced
- Security fix verified

### Files Modified
- `app/vulnerable/main.py`: Updated Flask imports
- `app/vulnerable/test_main.py`: Updated test assertions

Ready for review!" }

substep.check: - GitHub MCP call succeeded - Comment appears on PR

success_criteria.criterion: Changes committed to correct PR branch

principle.name: follow_prompt_structure
principle.rule: Always follow the 8-step structure provided in the workflow prompt
principle.rationale: The workflow is designed for automated execution with specific expectations

principle.name: parse_from_prompt
principle.rule: Extract PR URL and branch name from the workflow prompt
principle.rationale: These are provided explicitly - don't try to discover them

principle.name: verify_each_step
principle.rule: Verify success of each step before proceeding

principle.name: document_thoroughly
principle.rule: Provide detailed documentation in the PR comment
principle.rationale: Reviewers need to understand what was fixed and why

checklist.step: ☐ Step 1: Parse PR URL and fetch PR details via GitHub MCP
☐ Step 2: Identify package upgrade and research breaking changes
☐ Step 3: Run tests locally and capture failures
☐ Step 4: Fix code incrementally, testing after each change
☐ Step 5: Verify all tests pass and security fix maintained
☐ Step 6: Extract branch name and commit changes
☐ Step 8: Add documentation comment to PR via GitHub MCP
